stem: A political polling study surveys 30 registered voters and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 30 (13.3%). Estimate the true voter support for a policy and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample proportion: 0.1333. Wilson 95% CI: [0.0531, 0.2968]. Point estimate: 0.1749.

common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=30, the normal approximation may be poor
